entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal grain commonly grown for its seeds and stalks.

entities.name: ATHERIGONA SOCATA
entities.description: A species of shoot fly, a pest of sorghum.

entities.name: SHOOT FLY
entities.description: A pest that attacks the shoots of plants, specifically sorghum in this context.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand the harmful effects of a pest.

entities.name: SOLUBLE SUGARS
entities.description: Sugars that dissolve in water, found in plant tissues.

entities.name: LEAF SURFACE WETNESS
entities.description: The degree to which the surface of a leaf is covered in moisture.

entities.name: SEEDLING VIGOUR
entities.description: The early growth strength and health of a young plant.

entities.name: LEAF GLOSSINESS
entities.description: The shininess or reflectivity of a leaf surface.

entities.name: PLUMULE PIGMENTATION
entities.description: The coloration of the plumule, the embryonic shoot of a plant.

entities.name: LEAF SHEATH PIGMENTATION
entities.description: The coloration of the leaf sheath, the base of the leaf blade.

entities.name: TRICHOME DENSITY
entities.description: The number and distribution of trichomes (plant hairs) on a leaf surface.

entities.name: TANNIN
entities.description: A type of polyphenol found in plants, often associated with defense mechanisms.

entities.name: MAGNESIUM
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient, denoted as Mg.

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: An essential plant nutrient, denoted as Zn.

entities.name: STEPWISE REGRESSION
entities.description: A statistical method used to identify the most important predictors in a model.

entities.name: PATH COEFFICIENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to analyze the direct and indirect effects of variables.

entities.name: DAMAGE
entities.description: Harm or injury caused to the plant by the shoot fly.

relationships.weight: Susceptibility to shoot fly is linked to high levels of soluble sugars.
Susceptibility to shoot fly is linked to high levels of fats.
Susceptibility to shoot fly is linked to high leaf surface wetness.
Susceptibility to shoot fly is linked to high seedling vigour.
Leaf glossiness confers resistance to shoot fly.
Plumule pigmentation confers resistance to shoot fly.
Leaf sheath pigmentation confers resistance to shoot fly.
Trichome density confers resistance to shoot fly.
Tannin confers resistance to shoot fly.
Magnesium confers resistance to shoot fly.
Zinc confers resistance to shoot fly.
Stepwise regression explains variation in shoot fly damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Magnesium is important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Zinc is important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Soluble Sugars are important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Tannins are important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Fats are important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Leaf Glossiness is important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Leaf Sheath Pigmentation is important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Plumule Pigmentation is important for explaining damage.
Stepwise regression indicates Trichome Density is important for explaining damage.
Path coefficient analysis suggests leaf glossiness can be used as a marker trait.
Path coefficient analysis suggests trichome density can be used as a marker trait.
Path coefficient analysis suggests Magnesium can be used as a marker trait.
Path coefficient analysis suggests fat content can be used as a marker trait.
Path coefficient analysis suggests plumule pigmentation can be used as a marker trait.
Sorghum exhibits resistance to shoot fly.
